name: Reproduce CMB acoustic peak structure
requirement: Any candidate framework must reproduce the locations and amplitudes of the temperature and polarization acoustic peaks in the cosmic microwave background, as measured by Planck (and confirmed by ACT and SPT), at the per-mille level. The peak ratios fix the baryon density independently of BBN, the angular position of the first peak fixes the spatial geometry to be flat to ~0.4%, and the damping tail fixes the effective number of relativistic species N_eff = 2.99 +/- 0.17.

regime.description: Last-scattering surface at redshift z ~ 1100, temperature ~3000 K. Sound-horizon physics encoded in multipoles ell ~ 200 - 2500.

current_bound.experiment: Planck 2018 (TT,TE,EE+lowE+lensing) with consistency from ACT DR6 and SPT-3G
current_bound.reference: Planck Collaboration, 'Planck 2018 results. VI. Cosmological parameters', A&A 641, A6 (2020), arXiv:1807.06209

what_it_excludes: Frameworks that significantly modify recombination physics (e.g. early-dark-energy proposals beyond ~10% energy density at z ~ 3000), drastically change the sound horizon, or introduce extra relativistic species above |Delta N_eff| ~ 0.3 are excluded. The CMB damping tail in particular is a precision sieve for new light degrees of freedom in the early universe.

parameterization.name: LambdaCDM 6-parameter model (Omega_b h^2, Omega_c h^2, theta_MC, tau, A_s, n_s) plus optional extensions (Omega_K, Sum m_nu, w_0, w_a, N_eff, EDE fraction f_EDE and z_EDE, etc.).
parameterization.reference: Boltzmann codes: CAMB (Lewis-Challinor) and CLASS (Blas-Lesgourgues-Tram). Likelihood pipelines: cobaya, MontePython.
parameterization.summary: The CMB power spectra C_l^TT, C_l^TE, C_l^EE are computed by solving a coupled Boltzmann hierarchy through recombination plus an MCMC fit to Planck and ground-based data.

procedural.predicted_observable: Predicted C_l for ell ~ 2 - 2500 in temperature and polarization. Fit against Planck PR4 (or NPIPE) likelihoods plus ACT DR6 + SPT-3G high-l data.
framework_inputs: Modified Boltzmann hierarchy: any new species, modified gravity, or non-trivial dark sector must be expressible as additions to the perturbation equations.
Modified background expansion H(z) if the framework alters the cosmological background.
Modified recombination physics if the framework affects atomic transitions or photon decoupling.
Initial conditions for perturbations consistent with inflation or whatever the framework's early-universe theory is.
procedural.notes: CLASS and CAMB are the standard tools. Adding a new framework typically requires implementing it as a CLASS/CAMB module (a research-level engineering task per framework). Once implemented, the chi^2 against Planck/ACT/SPT data is mechanical.

primary_references.citation: Planck Collaboration, 'Planck 2018 results. VI. Cosmological parameters', A&A 641, A6 (2020)
ACT Collaboration, 'The Atacama Cosmology Telescope: DR6 Power Spectra, Likelihoods and LCDM Parameters' (2023)
SPT-3G Collaboration, 'Measurements of the E-mode polarization and temperature-E-mode correlation of the CMB from SPT-3G 2018 data', PRD 104, 022003 (2021)
